If the appeal panel finds that a designated operator or other person has not committed a violation, the panel must immediately inform the designated operator or other person, as the case may be, and the Minister of Transport of the finding.

Section.Text: The Minister of Transport may obtain from the Tribunal or the member, as the case may be, a certificate in the form established by the Governor in Council setting out the penalty required to be paid by the designated operator or other person that fails, within the time required,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to pay the penalty set out in the notice of violation or to file a request for a review under paragraph 129(2)(a); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to pay the amount determined under paragraph 131(b).

Subsection.Text: If the Minister of Transport offers to enter into a compliance agreement with the designated operator or other person, the agreement is subject to any terms that the Minister of Transport considers appropriate, including the reduction, in whole or in part, of the penalty for the violation.
If a compliance agreement is entered into, the designated operator or other person cannot file a request for a review under paragraph 129(2)(a).

A designated operator or other person that enters into a compliance agreement with the Minister of Transport is deemed to have committed the violation in respect of which the compliance agreement was entered into.

If the Minister of Transport is of the opinion that a designated operator or other person that has entered into a compliance agreement has complied with it, the Minister of Transport must serve a notice to that effect on the designated operator or other person and, on the service of the notice, the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended.

If the Minister of Transport is of the opinion that a designated operator or other person that has entered into a compliance agreement has not complied with it, the Minister of Transport must cause the designated operator to be served with a notice of default informing them that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: they are liable to pay, within the time and in the manner set out in the notice of default, the penalty set out in the notice of violation in respect of which the compliance agreement was entered into, less any amount they paid under the compliance agreement; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Transport may make public the designated operatorâs or other personâs name, the nature of the violation, the scope of the non-compliance with the compliance agreement and the penalty payable.

Subsection.Text: If a designated operator or other person pays the penalty set out in the notice of default within the time and in the manner set out in that notice, the Minister of Transport must accept the amount as complete satisfaction of the penalty owing in respect of the violation and the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended.
The Governor in Council may make regulations for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Act, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting cyber security programs;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting any condition and criteria respecting internal audits;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the form, manner and period for reporting any cyber security incidents referred to in section 17 and the types of incidents that must be reported;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the period within which a notification referred to under subsection 14(1) is to be provided;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the management of records referred to in section 30, including the collection, use, retention, disclosure and disposal of those records;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: designating any provision of this Act or of the regulations made under this Act for the purposes of section 90;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: classifying each violation as a minor violation, a serious violation or a very serious violation;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: fixing the maximum penalty in respect of each violation;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: defining, for the purposes of this Act, any word or expression that is used in this Act but is not defined; and

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing anything that is to be prescribed under this Act.

Subsection.Text: In making regulations under subsection (1), the Governor in Council may seek to ensure consistency with existing regulatory regimes, such as those established by provincial regulatory agencies.

Every person who contravenes section 10, subsection 13(1) or 14(1), section 17 or 18, subsection 30(1) or (2) or 32(4), section 35, subsection 37(1) or 41(4), section 44, subsection 46(1) or 50(4), section 53, subsection 55(1) or 59(4), section 62, subsection 64(1) or 68(4), section 71, subsection 74(1) or 78(4), section 81 or subsection 83(1) is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction.
Every person, partnership or unincorporated organization that contravenes section 29 is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction.

Section.Text: Every person who contravenes subsection 9(1), section 12 or 15, subsection 20(4), section 24, subsection 25(2), section 26 or 86 or paragraph 87(a) or (b) is guilty of an offence and liable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on summary conviction

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine in an amount that is in the discretion of the court or to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day, or to both, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a corporation, to a fine in an amount that is in the discretion of the court; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on conviction on indictment

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine in an amount that is in the discretion of the court or to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years, or to both, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a corporation, to a fine in an amount that is in the discretion of the court.

Section.Text: If a designated operator commits an offence under this Act, any director or officer of the designated operator that directed, authorized, assented to, acquiesced in or participated in the commission of the offence is a party to the offence and is liable on conviction to the punishment provided for by this Act, even if the designated operator is not prosecuted for or convicted of the offence.
If an offence under section 136 or 137 is committed or continued on more than one day, it constitutes a separate offence for each day on which the offence is committed or continued.
A prosecution must not be commenced in respect of an offence under this Act later than three years after the day on which the subject matter of the prosecution arose.

A person, partnership or unincorporated organization is not to be found guilty of an offence under this Act â other than an offence under section 137 that is in respect of a contravention of section 26 or paragraph 87(a) or (b) â if they establish that they exercised all due diligence to prevent the commission of the offence.

Section.MarginalNote: Offence by employee or agent or mandatary
Section.Text: In a prosecution under this Act, it is sufficient proof of an offence to establish that it was committed by an employee or agent or mandatary of the accused whether or not the employee or agent or mandatary is identified or has been prosecuted for the offence.

In any action or proceeding under this Act, any document purporting to be certified by a regulator as a true copy of a document made, given or issued under this Act is, without proof of the signature or of the official character of the person appearing to have signed the document,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: evidence of the original document of which it purports to be a copy;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: evidence of the fact that the original document was made, given or issued by or by the authority of or deposited with the person named in it and was made, given or issued at the time stated in the certified copy, if a time is stated in it; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: evidence of the fact that the original document was signed, certified, attested or executed by the persons and in the manner shown in the certified copy.

Section.Text: In any action or proceeding under this Act, an entry in any record required under this Act to be kept is, in the absence of evidence to the contrary, proof of the matters stated in it as against the person who made the entry or the designated operator that was required to keep the record.

Subsection.Text: The following rules apply to judicial review proceedings in respect of the issuance of a cyber security direction under section 20:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the judge determines that evidence or other information provided by the Minister is not relevant or if the Minister withdraws the evidence or other information, the decision of the judge must not be based on that evidence or other information and the judge must return it to the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the judge must ensure the confidentiality of all evidence and other information that the Minister withdraws.

Subsection.Text: In this section, judge means the Chief Justice of the Federal Court or a judge of that Court designated by the Chief Justice.

Section.Text: Section 145 applies to any appeal of a decision made by the judge in relation to the judicial review proceedings referred to in that section and to any further appeal, with any necessary modifications.

Subsection.Text: The Minister must, within three months after the end of each fiscal year, prepare a report on the administration of this Act for that fiscal year and cause a copy of the report to be laid before each House of Parliament on any of the first 15 sitting days of that House after the report is completed.
The report must include, for the fiscal year covered by the report, the following information in relation to orders made under subsection 20(1):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the number of orders made under subsection 20(1) and the nature of the directions set out in those orders;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the number of directions revoked under subsection 20(2);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the number of designated operators that were subject to a direction;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: description of compliance of designated operators that partially complied with a direction;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: description of compliance of designated operators that fully complied with a direction; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: an explanation of the necessity, proportionality, reasonableness and utility of the directions.

Subsection.Text: The report must contain information on, among other things,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the number of directions issued under subsection 20(1) in the immediately preceding fiscal year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the number of designated operators that were issued directions under subsection 20(1) in the immediately preceding fiscal year; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any other information relating to the immediately preceding fiscal year that the Minister considers relevant, if that information is not likely to be about an identifiable designated operator or other person.

Heading.TitleText: Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act

Section.Text: Subsection 23(1) of the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent shall, before December 31 in each year, ascertain the total amount of expenses incurred during the immediately preceding fiscal year for or in connection with the administration of the Bank Act, the Cooperative Credit Associations Act, the Critical Cyber Systems Protection Act, the Green Shield Canada Act, the Insurance Companies Act, the Protection of Residential Mortgage or Hypothecary Insurance Act and the Trust and Loan Companies Act.

ExplanatoryNote.TitleText: Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act
ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 12:

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent shall, before December 31 in each year, ascertain the total amount of expenses incurred during the immediately preceding fiscal year for or in connection with the administration of the Bank Act, the Cooperative Credit Associations Act, the Green Shield Canada Act, the Insurance Companies Act, the Protection of Residential Mortgage or Hypothecary Insurance Act and the Trust and Loan Companies Act.

Section.Text: The schedule to the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur la protection des cybersystÃ¨mes essentiels

Section.Text: Subsections 21(2) and (3) of the Nuclear Safety and Control Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Fees recoverable under any other Act of Parliament
Subsection.Text: The Commission may charge any fees that may be prescribed for any information, product or service that it provides under any other Act of Parliament.
The Commission may, under the prescribed circumstances, refund all or part of any fee referred to in paragraph (1)(g) or subsection (1.1).
The Commission may spend for its purposes the revenue from the fees it charges in the fiscal year in which the revenues are received or in the next fiscal year.
The Commission may, under the prescribed circumstances, refund all or part of any fee referred to in paragraph (1)(g).

The Commission may spend for its purposes the revenue from the fees it charges for licences or classes of licences issued under section 24 in the fiscal year in which the revenues are received or in the next fiscal year.

Heading.TitleText: Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada Act

Section.Text: Subsection 2(3) of the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Jurisdiction in respect of other Acts
Subsection.Text: The Tribunal also has jurisdiction in respect of reviews and appeals in connection with administrative monetary penalties provided for under sections 177 to 181 of the Canada Transportation Act, sections 127 to 133 of the Critical Cyber Systems Protection Act, sections 43 to 55 of the International Bridges and Tunnels Act, sections 129.01 to 129.19 of the Canada Marine Act, sections 16.1 to 16.25 of the Motor Vehicle Safety Act, sections 39.1 to 39.26 of the Canadian Navigable Waters Act and sections 130.01 to 130.19 of the Marine Liability Act.

ExplanatoryNote.TitleText: Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada Act
ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 15:

Subsection.Text: The Tribunal also has jurisdiction in respect of reviews and appeals in connection with administrative monetary penalties provided for under sections 177 to 181 of the Canada Transportation Act, sections 43 to 55 of the International Bridges and Tunnels Act, sections 129.01 to 129.19 of the Canada Marine Act, sections 16.1 to 16.25 of the Motor Vehicle Safety Act, sections 39.1 to 39.26 of the Canadian Navigable Waters Act and sections 130.01 to 130.19 of the Marine Liability Act.

Section.Text: The provisions of this Part come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

row.entry: Interprovincial or international pipeline and power line systems
Transportation systems that are within the legislative authority of Parliament

ScheduleFormHeading.Label: SCHEDULE 2
ScheduleFormHeading.TitleText: Classes of Operators and Corresponding Regulators
